nodes.title: Access the parent checkbox for indeterminate state
nodes.why: The indeterminate property is a live DOM property rather than a normal HTML attribute.
nodes.alternative: Represent partial selection with an indeterminate attribute in the template.
nodes.tradeoff: ViewChild introduces direct element access, but it is appropriate for this native checkbox property.

nodes.title: Apply parent selection to every child
nodes.why: Replacing the child-state array makes the parent toggle deterministic for both select-all and clear-all.
nodes.alternative: Update only the first child or derive child state from the parent element later.
nodes.tradeoff: The map allocates a small array, but Angular receives a clear state update.

nodes.title: Set the mixed parent state
nodes.why: A partially selected child set must clear checked and expose the native indeterminate visual state.
nodes.alternative: Treat any selected child as a fully checked parent.
nodes.tradeoff: The parent now has three visual states, which accurately communicates the child aggregate.
